## script to analysis the poms (only the pilot data) b cells in more detail
```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import scvi
import os
import celltypist
from celltypist import models
import re
```

```{python}
adata = sc.read_h5ad("poms.annotated.dan.h5ad")
```

## check the dataset
```{python}
list(adata.obs.columns)
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="basic_labels")
sc.pl.dotplot(adata, var_names=["IGHD", "IGHM", "CD27", "MME", "CR2", "CD24"], groupby="basic_labels")
```

## subset out the b cell clusters
```{python}
pom_b = adata[adata.obs["basic_labels"].isin(["Naive_B", "Memory_B", "Transitional_B"])].copy()
pom_b
del adata
```

## checking counts and logcounts data looks normal
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["counts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="blue", ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title("counts")

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["logcounts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="green", ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("logcounts")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## we will use scvi for batch correction (variational auto encoder deep learning method)
### set up the model, set the correct batch field
```{python}
batch_key = "patient_nice"
scvi.model.SCVI.setup_anndata(pom_b, layer="counts", batch_key=batch_key)
pom_b
model_scvi = scvi.model.SCVI(pom_b)
model_scvi.view_anndata_setup()
```

## set how many epochs (training rounds) and then train the model - this step is time consuming
```{python}
max_epochs_scvi = np.min([round((20000 / pom_b.n_obs) * 400), 400])
print(max_epochs_scvi)
model_scvi.train(early_stopping=True)
```

## asdd the scvi embedding to the object, then run clustering and plot umap
```{python}
pom_b.obsm["X_scANVI"] = model_scvi.get_latent_representation()
sc.pp.neighbors(pom_b, use_rep="X_scANVI")
sc.tl.umap(pom_b)
sc.pl.umap(pom_b, color=["patient_nice", "fetmat"])
```

```{python}
plt.rcParams['axes.titlesize'] = 32
sc.pl.umap(pom_b, color = ["CD19", "MS4A1", "IGHD", "IGHM", "IGHA1", "IGHA2", "IGHG1", "IGHG2", "IGHG3", "IGHG4", "CD27", "CR2", "CD24", "MME", "CXCR5", "ITGAX", "SPN", "CD38", "CD5", "CCR10"], layer = "logcounts", cmap="rocket", vmax = "p99", size = 50, legend_fontsize="small", save="_umap_markers.png")

sc.pl.umap(pom_b, color = ["fetmat", "gestation_week", "patient_nice"], layer = "logcounts", cmap="magma", vmax = "p99", size = 50, legend_fontsize="large", save="_umap_metadata.png", legend_loc="right margin", ncols = 1)

sc.pl.dotplot(pom_b, var_names=["IGHD", "IGHM", "IGHG1", "IGHG2", "IGHG3", "IGHG4", "IGHE", "IGHA1", "IGHA2", "CD27", "SPN", "CD38", "CD5", "CCR10"], groupby="basic_labels", save="_dotplot_markers.png")
plt.close('all')
```

## run celltypist to see if it can find B1 cells
### first download all models and pull out ones i want to use
```{python}
models.download_models(force_update = True)
model_low = models.Model.load(model="Immune_All_Low.pkl")
model_high = models.Model.load(model="Immune_All_High.pkl")
model_fetal = models.Model.load(model="Pan_Fetal_Human.pkl")
```

```{python}
print(model_low)
print(model_high)
print(model_fetal)
```


## i like to be able to see my obs columns separetly so making a df
```{python}
obs_df = pom_b.obs.copy()
```

```{python}
adata_low = pom_b.copy()
adata_high = pom_b.copy()
adata_fetal = pom_b.copy()

celltypist.annotate(adata_low, model="Immune_All_Low.pkl", majority_voting=True)
celltypist.annotate(adata_high, model="Immune_All_High.pkl", majority_voting=True)
celltypist.annotate(adata_fetal, model="Pan_Fetal_Human.pkl", majority_voting=True)
```
```{python}
sc.pl.umap(adata_low, color = ["majority_voting"])
sc.pl.umap(adata_high, color = ["majority_voting"])
sc.pl.umap(adata_fetal, color = ["majority_voting"])
```

```{python}
# Find all obs columns containing specific keywords
keywords = ["celltypist", "majority_voting", "predicted_labels"]

cols_to_remove = [col for col in pom_b.obs.columns if any(kw in col.lower() for kw in keywords)]

# Drop them
pom_b.obs.drop(columns=cols_to_remove, inplace=True)

# Check
print("Removed columns:", cols_to_remove)
print("Remaining columns:", pom_b.obs.columns)
```

```{python}
import celltypist
import pandas as pd

# Remove any old metadata columns that might interfere
keywords = ["celltypist", "predicted_labels", "majority_voting"]
cols_to_remove = [c for c in pom_b.obs.columns if any(kw in c.lower() for kw in keywords)]
pom_b.obs.drop(columns=cols_to_remove, inplace=True)

models_and_cols = [
    ("Immune_All_Low.pkl", "CT_low"),
    ("Immune_All_High.pkl", "CT_high"),
    ("Pan_Fetal_Human.pkl", "CT_fetal")
]

for model_file, col_name in models_and_cols:
    # Annotate and get results
    res = celltypist.annotate(pom_b, model=model_file, majority_voting=True)
    
    # Extract the predicted_labels DataFrame
    df_labels = res.predicted_labels  # this should be a pandas DataFrame
    
    # If majority_voting is present, you may choose to use it
    if "majority_voting" in df_labels.columns:
        sel = df_labels["majority_voting"]
    else:
        sel = df_labels["predicted_labels"]
    
    # Align and assign to pom_b.obs
    pom_b.obs[col_name] = sel.reindex(pom_b.obs_names).values
    
    print(f"{col_name} counts:")
    print(sel.value_counts().head())

# Then you can plot
import scanpy as sc
sc.pl.umap(pom_b, color=[c for _, c in models_and_cols], ncols=1, palette=["darkred", "dodgerblue", "forestgreen", "orange", "darkorchid"], save="celltypist_predictions.png")
```

```{python}
for res in [0.25,0.3,0.4,0.5,0.6,0.7,0.8,0.9,1,1.5,2]:
    sc.tl.leiden(pom_b, key_added = "subclustering_res" + str(res), resolution = res)

```

```{python}
sc.pl.umap(
    pom_b,
    color=["subclustering_res0.25", "subclustering_res0.5", "subclustering_res0.7", "subclustering_res1"], 
    size = 50, legend_fontsize="x-large", save="_resolutions.png", ncols=2
)
```

```{python}
sc.pl.dotplot(pom_b, var_names=["IGHD", "IGHM", "IGHG1", "IGHG2", "IGHG3", "IGHG4", "IGHE", "IGHA1", "IGHA2", "CD27", "SPN", "CD38", "CD5", "CCR10", "CR2", "CD24", "CXCR5", "ITGAX", "TBX21", "FAS"], standard_scale="var", dendrogram = True, swap_axes=True,
groupby="subclustering_res0.7", save="_resolution_0.7.dotplot_markers.png")
```
